## [03:00 - 06:01]

Now when you write the message make it super conversational. This is not an email. You're not emailing them. You're sending them a message. So treat it like you would a chat conversation on Messenger. Treat it like you would an SMS to a friend. That's how you need to come across because effectively in my opinion that's how you should be looking at this. You've created a friendship while it may be a business friendship. That's fine. So when you write the message it should be like hey I saw this awesome blog post on how to leverage Facebook ads for your business. What do you think about this? Or could you use this do you think? Are you doing anything like this? And definitely try to end these with a question because you want them to respond to you. And when they do they're just so much more likely to want to talk to you. So recently one of my clients sent a message to a fairly high level chief officer of some kind. I think he was a CEO or a VP at a relatively large company. So he sent this thank you message. This thanks for connecting message. And the other person said no thanks we're not interested. And he said okay I'm not sure to what you're referring but okay. Then the guy responded back to him and said hey sorry that was my executive assistant. You wouldn't believe how many pitches I get on LinkedIn. So he said no out of habit. I want you to let that sink in for a moment. Everyone is pitching people on LinkedIn. That is why we are not going to do that. The only thing that we're going to pitch them on LinkedIn is a quick get to know you call. I like to offer them like hey let's do a 15 minute call and see how we might be able to help each other. If you have a sales process set up already do not try to enter them into that sales process unless they ask to talk to you about hiring you. Then you can. Other than that just try to call them and get to know them. This is so and get to know them in a professional context. You don't need to ask about their wife or their kids. If it comes up talk about that. This is like this is online networking. So you definitely don't want to make it about you and what you do make it about them. They will appreciate that and they will naturally out of reciprocity kind of try to figure out what you do.

## [06:01 - 09:04]

And if you've positioned yourself well they're going to view you as an asset that they're going to try to figure out a leverage which is exactly what you want.
